subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **108 cards** total:
  - **96** numbered cards (1-12 in 4 colors, 2 of each)
  - **4** Skip cards
  - **8** Wild cards
- **Phase reference cards** (optional)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle** all 108 cards.
2. **Deal 10 cards** face down to each player.
3. **Place remaining cards** face down as the draw pile.
4. **Flip top card** to start the discard pile.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **10 cards in hand** — Keep hidden from other players.
- **All players start on Phase 1** — Track which phase each player is on.
- **Phase list** — All 10 phases must be completed in order.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Phase 10 is a rummy-style card game. Each player must complete 10 specific phases in order. The first player to complete all 10 phases wins. Only one phase can be completed per hand.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn:
1. **Draw** — Take the top card from either the draw pile or the discard pile.
2. **Lay down phase** (optional) — If you have all required cards for your current phase, lay them face up in front of you.
3. **Hit** (optional) — Add cards from your hand to any player's completed phase on the table.
4. **Discard** — Place 1 card on the discard pile to end your turn.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### The 10 Phases
1. **Phase 1** — 2 sets of 3 (three of a kind × 2)
2. **Phase 2** — 1 set of 3 + 1 run of 4
3. **Phase 3** — 1 set of 4 + 1 run of 4
4. **Phase 4** — 1 run of 7
5. **Phase 5** — 1 run of 8
6. **Phase 6** — 1 run of 9
7. **Phase 7** — 2 sets of 4
8. **Phase 8** — 7 cards of one color
9. **Phase 9** — 1 set of 5 + 1 set of 2
10. **Phase 10** — 1 set of 5 + 1 set of 3

#### Sets and Runs
- **Set** — Cards of the same number (color doesn't matter).
- **Run** — Consecutive numbered cards (color doesn't matter).

#### Wild Cards
- Can substitute for any number or color.
- Multiple wilds can be used in the same phase.

#### Skip Cards
- Discard a Skip card to skip the next player's turn.
- Cannot be picked up from the discard pile.
- Cannot be used in a phase.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **One phase per hand** — Even if you could complete two phases, you only advance one phase per round.
- **Must complete in order** — Cannot skip phases.
- **Failed phase** — If you don't complete your current phase, you attempt the same phase next hand.
- **Hitting** — After laying down your phase, you may add cards to any completed phase (yours or opponents').
- **Runs are expandable** — Add cards to either end of a run when hitting.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Round End
- A round ends when any player discards their last card (goes out).
- All other players score penalty points for cards remaining in hand.

#### Penalty Points
- **1-9** — 5 points each
- **10-12** — 10 points each
- **Skip cards** — 15 points each
- **Wild cards** — 25 points each

#### Game End
- Game ends when a player completes Phase 10 and goes out.
- If multiple players complete Phase 10 in the same round, lowest penalty score wins.
- Lower total score is better.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Focus on your phase** — Don't hold cards for future phases; complete the current one first.
- **Use Wilds wisely** — Save Wilds for hard-to-complete phases (long runs).
- **Go out quickly** — Once your phase is down, dump high-value cards by hitting others' phases.
- **Draw from discard** — Take the discard when it directly helps your phase.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Skip targeting** — Use Skip cards against the player in the lead.
- **Run preparation** — For long run phases, collect middle numbers (5-8) which can extend in either direction.
- **Minimize penalties** — If you won't complete your phase, focus on reducing cards in hand.
- **Wild conservation** — Wilds are most valuable for Phases 4-6 (long runs) and Phase 8 (7 of one color).
- **Pace awareness** — Track which phases opponents are on; falling behind is hard to recover from.
